sops.purpose: Create a navigable index of page types before deep skeleton work.
steps: Inventory page types and canonical URLs.
Create one standalone skeleton page per type.
Create one compare page per type.
Create one component-compare atlas per type.
Create one composition map per type.
Make index.html show the folder-style output tree and links.

sops.purpose: Turn a source URL into a static skeleton page fast without losing structure.
steps: Capture the full source at the locked viewport.
If full-page capture repeats, clips, or misses sections, use scroll-by-scroll slices with a manifest.
Map sections first, then components, states, and links.
Create the component compare atlas before refining the final page skeleton.
Skeletonize visible content by default.
Preserve component function and state before decorative accuracy.
Build the skeleton page and internal links.
Review in side-by-side page compare.
Run verifier checks before accepting the output.

sops.purpose: Record what the page is made of so templates can be unified across pages.
steps: List each section in scroll order.
List components inside each section.
Record repeated templates and card patterns.
Record selected, expanded, active, sticky, and disabled states.
Record links and expected navigation targets.
Call out special media behavior, such as animated or evolving blocks.

sops.purpose: Compare source and skeleton component by component.
steps: Crop source components from verified original screenshots only.
Crop matching skeleton components from the skeleton page.
If either source or skeleton full-page capture repeats, clips, or includes capture-only scrollbars, switch that side to scroll-by-scroll slices before cropping.
Stack each source/skeleton pair in a component atlas.
Output component-compare/{page}.html for every page type.
Include source coverage metadata with covered ranges or an explicit no-gap full-scroll marker.
Continue adding component rows until the source scroll depth is covered from top to bottom.
Verify the skeleton side has matching named component rows for the same scroll coverage, not repeated generic placeholders.
Write a component anatomy contract for each risky crop before skeletonizing: context/chrome, title/media/list/forms, repeated units, controls, and footnotes in source order.
Verify source and skeleton crops are not the same pixels.
Use the atlas to find missing subcomponents, wrong states, and rhythm errors.
Feed misses back into the composition map, skeleton template, and verifier.
Use approved components as a reusable library for stitching future page templates.

coreRules.name: viewport-lock
coreRules.rule: Use viewport 430x932 for mobile unless the user changes it.

coreRules.name: real-source-gate
coreRules.rule: Before any compare output is accepted, prove source screenshots or crops came from the original page, not the skeleton, compare page, or stale local output.

coreRules.name: scroll-slice-fallback
coreRules.rule: If source or skeleton full-page screenshots are incomplete, repeated, clipped, scrollbar-polluted, or visually suspect, switch that side to scroll-by-scroll slices and keep a manifest.

coreRules.name: stateful-media-block
coreRules.rule: If a hero or media area animates, evolves, or changes visual state while remaining one layout block, model it as one component with representative source states rather than separate sections.

coreRules.name: function-before-shape
coreRules.rule: Preserve function, state, and interaction groups before exact decorative shape.

coreRules.name: semantic-structure
coreRules.rule: Bullets, checks, numbered steps, icons, option cards, accordions, tabs, ratings, and CTA groups must remain distinct skeleton structures.

coreRules.name: component-anatomy-contract
coreRules.rule: For each risky component, record the ordered child anatomy before drawing the skeleton: context/chrome, title or media, paired media, lists or rows, controls, and footnotes. The skeleton must preserve that order.

coreRules.name: no-invented-structures
coreRules.rule: No invented structures: do not convert rows into cards, paired media into generic panels, or lists into paragraphs unless the source actually does that.

coreRules.name: row-count-gate
coreRules.rule: Row count gate: repeated semantic units must match source count and rhythm. Three stat rows, benefit rows, reviews, or FAQ rows become the same count unless intentionally grouped and labeled.

coreRules.name: paired-micro-content
coreRules.rule: Preserve paired data groups as components, such as review count plus rating, price plus option label, or label plus value.

coreRules.name: uniform-repeats
coreRules.rule: Repeated items in the same component family use one locked template, with variation only where the source pattern varies.

coreRules.name: local-gap-map
coreRules.rule: For key components, capture vertical rhythm between labels, links, CTAs, cards, and section edges.

coreRules.name: navigation-baked-in
coreRules.rule: Clickable skeleton elements should route to the matching internal skeleton page when the source has a clear page-to-page behavior.

coreRules.name: component-atlas-first
coreRules.rule: For new pages, create the component compare atlas before treating the standalone skeleton page as final.

coreRules.name: full-scroll-atlas-coverage
coreRules.rule: Component atlases must cover the whole source scroll depth. Use exact components where known; use named deep-section group components when a section is too large or not yet fully templated.

coreRules.name: matched-skeleton-coverage
coreRules.rule: The skeleton side of a component atlas must expose matching named component rows for the same full-scroll composition. Reusing broad generic blocks after a major section is a process failure even when the approximate vertical length matches.

specialCases.name: animated-home-hero
specialCases.appliesTo: METHODIQ home page and similar pages
specialCases.rule: Treat the hero as one evolving media block. Capture at least the initial state and one later state, then skeletonize as a single hero component with media area, overlay/status chips if present, metric cards if present, and persistent CTA.
